danger_signals.meaning: Obstacle/threat to player progress; must be outrun or rammed off-road

danger_signals.visual_form: On-screen gauge visual indicator moving toward 'OVERHEAT!' state
danger_signals.meaning: Turbo boost overuse; player loses turbo capability temporarily if gauge maxes

danger_signals.visual_form: Silver Porsche 959 traveling ahead on race route
danger_signals.meaning: Competitive threat; if opponent reaches sub-goal first, girlfriend character transfers to opponent's vehicle

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental boundaries and collision targets visible along race path
danger_signals.meaning: Ramming targets for police; environmental hazards that slow/damage player

safety_signals.visual_form: Checkpoint locations after approximately four cities traversed
safety_signals.meaning: Opportunity to improve vehicle attributes (Hi-Power Engine, Special Turbo, Super Grip Tires)

safety_signals.visual_form: Vehicle advancing from New York City toward Los Angeles on predefined single path
safety_signals.meaning: Successful navigation; linear route provides clear direction
Safety window for race completion; players aware of progress toward deadline

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Male driver with girlfriend passenger; racing from NYC to LA

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Carries girlfriend passenger when winning against player
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Travels same NYC-to-LA route; reaches sub-goals in competition
Spawn periodically during race; attempt to stop player progress

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Girlfriend remains in player's Ferrari F40; visual confirmation of lead
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: CPU opponent operates at reduced threat level; girlfriend does not transfer

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Girlfriend character moves to opponent's Porsche 959; visual transfer of passenger
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must catch opponent at next sub-goal checkpoint to reclaim girlfriend and bonus (1,000,000 points)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Girlfriend kisses driver in front of CPU opponent; visual celebration of victory
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: 1,000,000 point bonus awarded; final checkpoint reached triggers ending sequence

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Engine temperature gauge fills toward OVERHEAT; player speed increases visually
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Vehicle acceleration; gauge management required to avoid overheating penalty
Turbo boost becomes unavailable until temperature decreases; forced speed reduction

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Checkpoint location appears after traversing approximately four cities
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player stops; upgrade menu presents three vehicle attribute options (Hi-Power Engine, Special Turbo, Super Grip Tires)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Police vehicles appear on racing path
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must either outrun using turbo boost or ram police vehicles off-road into environmental objects

environmental_storytelling.example: Linear path from New York City to Los Angeles with intermediate city checkpoints; shows player journey across continental United States
Player drives Ferrari F40 (luxury/performance); CPU opponent drives Porsche 959 (silver, rival identity); visual differentiation of competing factions
Girlfriend presence in car serves dual purpose: romantic subplot (can be 'won' or 'lost' to CPU opponent) and visual indicator of competitive standing
At sub-goals, player chooses vehicle improvements; visual representation of strategic choices affecting race capability

environmental_storytelling.technique: Police presence as law enforcement context
environmental_storytelling.example: Occasional police cars suggest hazardous racing activity in public roads; world state where law enforcement opposes player activity

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Receives girlfriend passenger; gains visual confidence indicator; threat level increases
Loses girlfriend passenger to player; visual disadvantage; continues race but with diminished narrative status
Attempt to intercept and stop player; pursue on-road or force off-road; can be rammed into obstacles
Speed increase; engine temperature gauge rises; visual acceleration on screen
Turbo boost disabled; speed limited to normal; visual gauge warning state; forced cooldown period
Sits alongside driver; represents winning status; transfers to opponent if CPU reaches sub-goal first
Visual indicator of player's competitive loss; creates incentive for player to catch opponent at next checkpoint

notes: Turbo OutRun uses a racing paradigm with competitive opponent AI as primary world-state communicator. The girlfriend character functions as a dynamic narrative element that visually represents competitive standing. Danger signals are primarily mechanical (turbo overheat) and competitive (CPU opponent advancement). The game communicates faction identity through vehicle choice (Ferrari vs. Porsche) and color differentiation. Police presence suggests illicit street racing context. World state changes manifest through passenger location, vehicle positioning, and gauge states. Linear single-path route removes choice-based world-state branching present in original Out Run.
